articles.summary: Trump says Xi agrees Iran must not obtain nuclear weapon US President Donald Trump said on Friday that Chinese President Xi Jinping agreed that Iran should not possess a nuclear weapon and stressed that the Strait of Hormuz would remain open. Trump’s comments came as Tehran confirmed that United States officials had signalled readiness to continue negotiations, while Iranian foreign minister Abbas Araghchi said Iran remained open to any role China could play in a future settlement. articles.title: Long Island Rail Workers Strike in First Walkout Since 1994
articles.summary: The Long Island Rail Road, the nation’s busiest commuter line, will suspend service for the first time in more than 30 years after labor groups and transit officials failed to reach a deal on wage increases by a Friday night deadline.

articles.title: UAE foreign minister meets Reform UK leader Nigel Farage
articles.summary: UAE foreign minister meets Reform UK leader Nigel Farage Abdullah bin Zayed Al Nahyan met Nigel Farage during a visit to the United Kingdom, according to a statement released by the Ministry of Foreign Affairs of the United Arab Emirates. The Emirati ministry said the two discussed the “latest regional developments and the repercussions of the unprovoked Iranian terrorist attacks targeting the UAE”, as well as strategic relations between the UAE and Britain. The meeting took place as part of a broader visit by bin Zayed to the UK, during which he also met British Foreign Secretary David Lammy.

articles.summary: Iranian diplomat says solidarity with Palestinians is not enough Iran’s ambassador to the United Nations, Amir Saeid Iravani, said support for the Palestinian cause must go beyond expressions of solidarity and include efforts to address what he described as the root causes of Palestinian suffering under Israeli occupation. Speaking at a United Nations event marking the 78th anniversary of the Nakba on Friday, Iravani called for “international mobilisation” and a more “action-oriented approach” from the global community. articles.title: Israeli forces and settlers carry out raids across West Bank
articles.summary: Israeli forces and settlers carry out raids across West Bank Israeli forces and settlers carried out raids and attacks across several towns and villages in the occupied West Bank on Friday night, Wafa news agency reported.  The reported incidents took place in the governorates of Salfit, Hebron, Ramallah, Bethlehem and Jenin. In the town of Az-Zawiya west of Salfit, Israeli troops raided homes, searched residents and closed the main street. articles.title: China criticises US-backed Hormuz resolution at UN
articles.summary: China criticises US-backed Hormuz resolution at UN Fu Cong criticised a proposed US and Bahrain-backed resolution at the United Nations Security Council concerning the Strait of Hormuz, saying both the content and timing of the measure were inappropriate. The draft resolution calls on Iran to halt attacks and mining operations in the waterway, but diplomats say it is likely to face vetoes from China and Russia if brought to a vote. articles.title: Trump says Iran will not use Hormuz ‘as a weapon’
articles.summary: Trump says Iran will not use Hormuz ‘as a weapon’ US President Donald Trump said the Strait of Hormuz “will be open” and vowed that Iran would not be allowed to use the strategic waterway “as a weapon”. Speaking to Fox News, Trump said: “The Strait of Hormuz will be open and we will ensure that the Iranians do not possess nuclear weapons and that the world remains stable.” Trump also said China depended on the strait for 40 percent of its oil supplies and accused Tehran of previously closing the waterway to exert pressure. “Iran has closed the Strait of Hormuz in the past and used it as a weap

articles.title: US congresswoman says ‘Nakba did not end in 1948’
articles.summary: US congresswoman says ‘Nakba did not end in 1948’ Palestinian American lawmaker Rashida Tlaib commemorated the Nakba in remarks delivered before the US Congress, saying the displacement of Palestinians during the creation of Israel in 1948 continues to shape events today. The US congresswoman referred to the expulsion of an estimated 750,000 Palestinians during the war surrounding Israel’s founding. “We know the Nakba did not end in 1948,” Tlaib said. “We all have witnessed it live. It continues to this day as the Israeli government commits genocide in Gaza, forcibly displacing Palestinians an

articles.title: US senators criticise Trump over economic impact of Iran war
articles.summary: US senators criticise Trump over economic impact of Iran war Democratic lawmakers criticised President Donald Trump’s war on Iran, saying the conflict was driving up fertiliser, fuel and household costs across the United States. Senator Tammy Baldwin wrote on X: “President Trump’s war of choice in Iran has sent the cost of fertilizer and gas through the roof for our farmers.” Senator Mark Warner, chairman of the Senate Intelligence Committee, said, “From gas prices to home costs, Trump’s war of choice is making it harder and harder to afford the life you want.” Senator Mark Kelly also criticis

articles.summary: Lebanon and Israel agree to extend ceasefire for 45 days Lebanon welcomed a 45-day extension of the ceasefire with Israel following two days of negotiations in Washington, even as Israeli attacks continued across Lebanon, killing at least 11 people on Wednesday. In a statement shared by the Lebanese presidency, Beirut said the renewed truce could ease pressure on civilians and help support stability, while calling for an independent monitoring mechanism and firm guarantees from the United States. articles.summary: Hezbollah says it launched 33 attacks on Israeli forces in south Lebanon Hezbollah said its fighters carried out 33 attacks against Israeli soldiers operating inside southern Lebanon on Friday. According to Hezbollah, the attacks targeted Israeli troop concentrations and armoured vehicles across several border villages and ravines in southern Lebanon.
